video:video.video:title: Images released by Royal Thai Navy shows Thai cargo ship set ablaze in Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Authorities in Thailand have released images of the Thai cargo ship that was struck and set ablaze in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Iran says it can't participate in the FIFA World Cup amid war with U.S. and Israel
video:video.video:description: Iran's sports minister said Wednesday that the country cannot participate in the 2026 FIFA World Cup amid war with the U.S and Israel.

video:video.video:title: Iranian drone hits Dubai tower as attacks reported across Gulf states
video:video.video:description: Firefighters extinguished a blaze in two units at the Address Creek 2 Tower. The high-rise building showed some damage to its upper floors.

video:video.video:title: Hockney's 70 metre Bayeux tapestry-inspired artwork opens in London
video:video.video:description: An epic 70 metre art work by David Hockney, inspired by the Bayeux tapestry and the changing seasons in Normandy, is the star of a new landmark exhibition in London.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 12
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, March 12th: Iran strikes Bahrain’s International Airport; U.S. releases more video of strikes in Iran; Trump comments on war; Cambodia shuts down 200 scam centers.

video:video.video:title: Tiny rescue pony wins hearts after recovering from illness
video:video.video:description: A miniature pony who once struggled even to stand is now trotting toward a much brighter future at a rescue centre in northwest England.

video:video.video:title: Slave ship remnant to leave Smithsonian as it impacts visitors in final month
video:video.video:description: The National Museum of African American History and Culture is removing a rare slave ship timber from its Slavery and Freedom exhibit and sending it back to South Africa.
video:video.video:tag: National Museum of African American History and Culture

video:video.video:title: Attacks targeted oil ship near southern port in Basra
video:video.video:description: An attack on Iraq’s Basra port early Thursday killed at least one person and forced authorities to halt operations at all the country’s oil terminals, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Bahrain firefighters extinguish blaze at oil tanker on Muharraq Island after Iranian attack
video:video.video:description: Firefighters in Bahrain worked to extinguish an oil tanker fire on Thursday on Muharraq Island near the nation's International Airport.

video:video.video:title: Smoke rises from the Harir base after attacks in the region
video:video.video:description: Smoke rose from the Harir base in northern Iraq on Thursday after attacks targeted the region. Harir air base was used by US and Turkish forces during the international coalitions’ war against the Islamic State militants in Iraq but was evacuated last year.

video:video.video:title: Is Atlanta still the ‘Hollywood of the South’? A film boomtown faces a test
video:video.video:description: After hitting a peak of $4.4 billion in 2022, spending on film and TV production in Georgia has tumbled.

video:video.video:title: Restaurants providing less food to reach more customers
video:video.video:description: Some restaurants are offering smaller portion sizes on their menus for people using weight-loss drugs who just aren’t hungry enough.

video:video.video:title: Tornadoes kill 2 in northwestern Indiana and raze buildings in Kankakee, Illinois
video:video.video:description: Major storms whipped up tornadoes that killed at least two people in northwest Indiana and leveled buildings in Kankakee, Illinois.

video:video.video:title: 'Imperfect Women' cast celebrates imperfections
video:video.video:description: Kerry Washington, Elisabeth Moss and Kate Mara, who take the lead roles in new thriller 'Imperfect Women,' are willing to tell the "tooth" about their own imperfections.

video:video.video:title: Celebrity chef René Redzepi resigns from iconic Danish restaurant Noma after abuse allegations
video:video.video:description: René Redzepi, founder and celebrity chef at the iconic Danish restaurant Noma, has stepped down from his post following allegations of abuse and assault at the Copenhagen landmark.

video:video.video:title: Iran continues unrelenting attacks on shipping, energy infrastructure in Persian Gulf
video:video.video:description: A widening war in Iran has halted oil tankers, made targets of refineries and spooked investors worried about the cascading impact of spiking energy prices.

video:video.video:title: Ohio State names provost as its new president after predecessor's abrupt resignation
video:video.video:description: Ohio State has moved past the abrupt resignation of the university’s president by elevating its chief academic officer into the role.

video:video.video:title: Senators seek further investigation into deadly Iran school attack
video:video.video:description: U.S. Senators are demanding answers from the Trump administration as a growing body of evidence suggests the United States carried out a deadly missile strike on an elementary school in Iran that killed over 165 people, many of them children.

video:video.video:title: Iran's supreme leader says closure of Strait of Hormuz should be used as leverage
video:video.video:description: Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei issued Thursday his first statement on the war, saying that the leverage of closing the Strait of Hormuz should be used, and that attacks on Iran’s Gulf Arab neighbors will continue.

video:video.video:title: Giant steel pipe’s mysterious overnight growth spurt baffles a Japanese city
video:video.video:description: A giant underground pipe rose more than 32 feet out of a construction site in a busy area of the Japanese city of Osaka overnight, nearly reaching an elevated road without any witnesses.

video:video.video:title: US Central Command releases video of strikes on Iranian planes, boats, trucks
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military’s Central Command published footage showing what it said was strikes targeting Iranian military aircraft, trucks and boats.

video:video.video:title: Film cameras are trendy again, here's how to use one
video:video.video:description: Film cameras are trendy again, here's how to use one. (AP video by Lorna Petty)

video:video.video:title: Trump asked on possible Iran revenge plot in California: "It's being investigated"
video:video.video:description: A reporter asked President Donald Trump on Wednesday about a 'bulletin that some law enforcement put out about a possible Iran revenge plot in California.'

video:video.video:title: Steel pipe protrudes 13 meters above ground on a highway in Osaka
video:video.video:description: A giant steel pipe protruded from the ground on Wednesday on a highway in Osaka city in western Japan, rising as high as 13 metres above ground, according to local authorities.

video:video.video:title: Hawaii lei face competition from cheaper imports
video:video.video:description: The flowers in that bright-purple lei you may have received on your Hawaii vacation almost certainly weren't grown in the Aloha State.

video:video.video:title: Major seaside tourist area of Beirut hit by an Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: An Israeli strike hit a car Thursday in Ramlet al-Bayda, a major seaside tourist area of Beirut where dozens of displaced people have been sheltering.

video:video.video:title: California officials downplay information that Iran may be targeting their state with drone strikes
video:video.video:description: California officials say they are aware of information in various news reports that Iran could be targeting the U.S. with drone strikes in retaliation for the ongoing war in Iran but many are downplaying that threat.

video:video.video:title: Displaced Palestinians in Gaza return to a tent camp that was struck by Israel the day before
video:video.video:description: Displaced Palestinians sheltering west of Gaza City returned Thursday morning to a tent camp struck by Israel the day prior.

video:video.video:title: Teyana Taylor's One Fabulous Look After Another
video:video.video:description: Teyana Taylor has been a standout on the red carpet this awards season, even bringing her young daughter to help style her at the Actor Awards.

video:video.video:title: Old Dominion shooting suspect is ex-Army National Guard member with past terror conviction
video:video.video:description: Police stood guard Thursday afternoon outside a building on the campus of Old Dominion University in Virginia, where authorities say the shooter killed one person and injured two others.

video:video.video:title: Police responds to scene after armed man rams vehicle into Michigan synagogue
video:video.video:description: A man armed with a rifle rammed a vehicle into a major synagogue in a Detroit suburb and was fatally shot by security.

video:video.video:title: Beirut branch of financial arm hit by Israel airstrikes had been closed, Hezbollah official says
video:video.video:description: A Hezbollah official said Thursday that a building in the Zoqaq el-Blat neighborhood of Beirut that Israel alleged was a branch of Hezbollah's financial arm and was damaged in Israeli airstrikes had been closed for "two weeks."

video:video.video:title: Reaction as Noma celebrity chef René Redzepi resigns over abuse allegations
video:video.video:description: René Redzepi, the founder and celebrity chef at the iconic Danish restaurant Noma that won three Michelin stars and other international accolades for its innovative “New Nordic” cuisine, has stepped down following allegations of abuse and assault at the Copenhagen landmark.

video:video.video:title: Associated Press reporter gives details on scene of Michigan synagogue attack
video:video.video:description: A man armed with a rifle rammed his vehicle into one of the nation’s largest reform synagogues in a Detroit suburb and was fatally shot by security Thursday, The Associated Press has learned.

video:video.video:title: Some Indian eateries forced to close due to shortage of cooking gas
video:video.video:description: Restaurant owners in India say they are struggling to secure liquefied petroleum gas cylinders. Much of the gas is imported, traveling through the Strait of Hormuz, which is effectively closed due to the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Studies show penguins are attracted to harmful white plastics
video:video.video:description: Researchers investigating the impact of plastic on seabirds say their study shows penguins are more attracted to the color white.

video:video.video:title: Oil jumps to $100 per barrel and stocks sink worldwide
video:video.video:description: With no clear end in sight, the war with Iran is sending oil prices back to $100 per barrel, and stocks are sinking worldwide on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un and his teenage daughter shoot guns on visit to weapons factory
video:video.video:description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un and his teenage daughter fired pistols during an inspection of a light munitions factory.

video:video.video:title: Casts of Pompeii victims captured at the time of their death goes on display
video:video.video:description: For the first time, more than 20 casts of those who died in the catastrophic volcano eruption in Pompeii in 79 AD, are going on display together in a new, permanent exhibition.

video:video.video:title: Proud, ‘Imperfect Women’ star in new thriller
video:video.video:description: After playing best friends on screen in new thriller series "Imperfect Women," actors Kerry Washington, Elisabeth Moss and Kate Mara discuss the pressure to be perfect and their own friendship groups.

video:video.video:title: Smaller portions trend as diners watch budgets and waistlines
video:video.video:description: Restaurants are rolling out smaller, cheaper menu options as diners look to spend less, eat lighter or manage weight, a shift reflecting changing attitudes about food and portion sizes.

video:video.video:title: Market swings? Advisors say stick to your plan, not your emotions
video:video.video:description: When stock markets are falling, it’s natural to want to do something quickly to protect your savings. Historically, though, staying calm has been best when investing.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 12
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, March 12th: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei issues first statement; charges dropped against Israeli soldiers accused of sexually assaulting Palestinian detainees; man with a riffle dead after ramming into Michigan synagogue and U.S.

video:video.video:title: Cyber threats rise as Iran-linked hackers eye US targets
video:video.video:description: Pro-Iranian hackers are targeting sites in the Middle East and starting to stretch into the United States during the war.

video:video.video:title: NASA clears its Artemis moon rocket for an April launch with four astronauts following repairs
video:video.video:description: NASA cleared its moon rocket on Thursday for an April launch with four astronauts after completing the latest round of repairs. (AP video by Javier Arciga)

video:video.video:title: Paris voters head to polls to elect new mayor
video:video.video:description: Parisian voters will head to the polls Sunday to elect their mayor in a closely watched race that could reshape politics in the French capital.

video:video.video:title: Israeli military says attacks on Iran are "expanding every day"
video:video.video:description: An Israeli military spokesman on Thursday said attacks on Iran are "expanding every day.” U.S. and Israeli strikes have exacted a heavy toll on Iran’s leadership, military and ballistic missile program, but have failed to topple the government, which U.S.

video:video.video:title: How rising oil prices from the Iran war could drive up everyday costs
video:video.video:description: The war in Iran is pushing crude oil prices higher, and that could mean rising costs for gas, heating and even groceries.

video:video.video:title: From mini-sabbaticals to adult gap years, more people are taking extended work breaks
video:video.video:description: From mini-sabbaticals to adult gap years, some workers are stepping away from their jobs for months to travel, pursue passions or recover from burnout.

video:video.video:title: Hockey player who lost family in Rhode Island shooting scores winning goal in double overtime
video:video.video:description: Nearly a month after losing three family members in a Rhode Island ice rink shooting, Colin Dorgan scored a game-winning goal in double overtime that helped advance the Blackstone Valley Co-op out of the semifinals.

video:video.video:title: Funeral in Baghdad for PMF fighters killed by airstrike on western Iraq
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of people participated in the funeral procession held in Baghdad for members of the Popular Mobilization Forces, or PMF, who were killed by an airstrike in Anbar province on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Thousands march to US Embassy in Athens to protest ongoing attacks on Iran
video:video.video:description: Several thousand demonstrators in Greece marched to the U.S. Embassy in Athens on Thursday to protest against ongoing attacks on Iran. (AP Video by Derek Gatopoulos)

video:video.video:title: AP explains closing of China's annual NPC
video:video.video:description: China's National People's Congress closed on Thursday with a final session in Beijing, overseen by Chinese President Xi Jinping.

video:video.video:title: Attack in northern Israel leaves several injured, emergency services say
video:video.video:description: Israel's emergency services, Magen David Adom, said some 30 people had been hurt in a missile attack on Zarzir, about 30 kilometres from Haifa, in northern Israel.

video:video.video:title: Quds Day rallies take place in Tehran, Isfahan and Mashhad in Iran
video:video.video:description: Iranian state television aired footage showing thousands of people across the Islamic Republic taking part in the annual Quds Day.

video:video.video:title: Louisiana's alligator farms mix capitalism with conservation
video:video.video:description: Advocates say commercial alligator farming has helped preserve the species. Not all conservationists think that's a good thing, but for the farmers and luxury brands seeking to market their products as sustainable, it's made sense to tie conservation to capitalism.

video:video.video:title: 2 Ohio pastors emerge as faithful allies for Haitian migrants during Trump's crackdown
video:video.video:description: One grew up in rural Haiti amid the poverty and violence. The other grew up in Michigan as a self-described “blue-collar farm kid."

video:video.video:title: Snow geese take off for the Arctic in mesmerizing sunrise display
video:video.video:description: Thousands of snow geese touch down at a Pennsylvania reservoir each spring, turning sunrise into a loud, swirling liftoff that draws big crowds.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth says Iran's new supreme leader is 'disfigured' but didn't provide details
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is wounded and 'likely disfigured.'

video:video.video:title: Large explosion in Iran's capital, state TV reports
video:video.video:description: A large explosion struck Iran's capital, Tehran, on Friday Iranian state television reported. The explosion hit Ferdowsi Square, just down Enghelab Street from Tehran University, the epicenter of ongoing Quds, or Jerusalem Day demonstrations.

video:video.video:title: How to use a film camera
video:video.video:description: As young people embrace film, analog cameras are not super easy to use. What does ISO mean? What is an f/stop anyway? How do I figure out the right aperture?

video:video.video:title: ROTC students at Old Dominion subdued and killed the shooter who killed 1 person, wounded 2
video:video.video:description: The FBI says ROTC students in an Old Dominion University classroom subdued and killed the shooter who killed one person and wounded two other.

video:video.video:title: Iranian ambassador to Tunisia says new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is ‘safe and in good health'
video:video.video:description: Iran’s ambassador to Tunisia mourned Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at a press conference Thursday and said the new Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, the slain leader’s son, was “safe and in good health” following reports that he had been wounded in a strike at the start of the Iran

video:video.video:title: Refusing to answer questions on Iran school strike, Hegseth says officer picked to lead investigation
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says U.S. Central Command has picked a lead officer to investigate a deadly strike on a school in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Indiana man struggles to get a job as labor market remains under strain
video:video.video:description: Since losing his job last November, 25-year-old John Sand said he has applied to nearly 300 jobs, from social media roles to Amazon warehouses, but hasn’t found work yet.

video:video.video:title: Two long-lost episodes of 'Doctor Who' found
video:video.video:description: A film charity announced Friday that it has found two previously lost 1960s episodes of iconic sci-fi TV series "Doctor Who" among the possessions of a deceased collector.

video:video.video:title: A gay Muslim influencer in Germany calls for tolerance during Ramadan
video:video.video:description: Ali Darwich, a gay Muslim influencer, picks up a date from his plate, takes a sip of water, and addresses the 15 friends sitting around the table and breaking the Ramadan fast with him.

video:video.video:title: The top photos of the week by Associated Press Photojournalists
video:video.video:description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most compelling images from around the world, published by The Associated Press during the week of 6-12 March, 2026

video:video.video:title: A record number of political parties register for Haiti's first election in a decade
video:video.video:description: A record 280 political parties and counting had registered by Thursday’s deadline to participate in Haiti’s first general election in a decade, although not all will make the cut.

video:video.video:title: Coco Jones reveals what Ciara taught her
video:video.video:description: At an Essence Black Women in Hollywood event in Los Angeles, Coco Jones revealed that her best mentor has been Ciara.

video:video.video:title: Sheriff says driver who rammed into Michigan synagogue is dead
video:video.video:description: The Associated Press has learned that a man armed with a rifle who rammed his vehicle into a major synagogue in a Detroit suburb was fatally shot by a security guard (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Paralympians ski in shorts and T-shirts, and some ask: Shouldn’t these Games be earlier?
video:video.video:description: At the Paralympics, some athletes competed in shorts and T-shirts. The warmth is increasingly commonplace in March as temperatures rise.

video:video.video:title: In Louisiana, alligator farms mix capitalism and conservation
video:video.video:description: A Louisiana farm that raises alligators for skins used in luxury goods also helps conserve the species.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 13
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, March 13th: Hegseth says Iran's new leader is wounded; Israeli airstrikes hit Beirut suburb; Record number of political parties register for Haiti's election; Russian crews set off explosives to break up ice jams

video:video.video:title: Workers search rubble of damaged buildings after at least 8 people killed in Israeli strike in Sidon
video:video.video:description: At least eight people have been killed in an Israeli strike in the southern coastal city of Sidon, Lebanon’s Health Ministry said Friday. Nine others were wounded.

video:video.video:title: Come with me to the Governors Ball preview
video:video.video:description: The Governors Ball is where Oscar winners and nominees will party after the ceremony, and AP got a sneak peek. Follow Sarah Jones-Smith inside.

video:video.video:title: Controlled explosions carried out to prevent ice jams on Russian rivers and reduce spring floods
video:video.video:description: A military engineering team of the Novosibirsk missile formation carried out controlled explosions Friday on a tributary of the Ob River in Russia's Siberia to prevent the formation of ice jams and reduce the risk of spring flooding.

video:video.video:title: Ready set roll! Conan O'Brien unveils Oscars red carpet
video:video.video:description: Oscars host Conan O'Brien rolls out the red carpet ahead of Sunday's event. The ceremony airs at 7 p.m. ET on ABC and will stream on Hulu for the first time.

video:video.video:title: Cuban president says talks were recently held with the US to resolve differences
video:video.video:description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel said Friday that his government has held recent talks with the U.S., marking the first time the Caribbean country confirms such speculation.

video:video.video:title: An inside look at the Oscars Rolex Greenroom
video:video.video:description: Before and after hitting the Academy Awards stage, Hollywood's biggest stars can be found chatting backstage in the Oscars Greenroom hosted by watch maker Rolex.

video:video.video:title: Workers prepare Oscars red carpet ahead of Sunday ceremony
video:video.video:description: Workers are putting the finishing touches on the Oscars red carpet ahead of Sunday's Academy Awards ceremony in Hollywood, to be hosted by Conan O'Brien. (March 13)

video:video.video:title: Michael B. Jordan on portraying twins in 'Sinners'
video:video.video:description: Journey to the Oscars: At the "Sinners" premiere last year, Michael B. Jordan explained the preparation that went into portraying his twin characters, Smoke and Stack.

video:video.video:title: Friend of ROTC leader killed in Old Dominion University shooting visits campus to honor him
video:video.video:description: Eddie Flack expressed his respect and appreciation for the men and women who serve in the military to protect our country, “It's just unfortunate that they have to fight a two-front war, domestic and abroad.”

video:video.video:title: 'One Battle After Another' stars on film's impact
video:video.video:description: Journey to the Oscars: Regina Hall, Teyana Taylor and Chase Infiniti say they wanted to start conversations with “One Battle After Another.”

video:video.video:title: Ryan Coogler 'not thinking about' potential Oscars history
video:video.video:description: Ryan Coogler could become the first Black filmmaker to win best director in the Academy Awards’ nearly century-long history, but says he's focused on the 'Sinners' team before Sunday's ceremony.

video:video.video:title: Cuban president confirms talks with US government to find solutions to problems given their severity and impact
video:video.video:description: In what some are calling a historic moment for Cuba-U.S. relations, and after days of intense speculation, Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel has confirmed that talks between Cuba and the United States have taken place.

video:video.video:title: How higher oil prices can affect your wallet
video:video.video:description: Oil prices are rising which makes the costs of gasoline, diesel and jet fuel more expensive. But you don’t have to drive a car to be affected

video:video.video:title: US forecasts blizzard, polar vortex, heat dome and atmospheric river all at once
video:video.video:description: The United States is getting slammed by a stretch of March weather extremes, from flooding rain to record heat and late-season snow.

video:video.video:title: How warm weather affects racing at the Winter Paralympics
video:video.video:description: At the Winter Paralympics, some athletes competed in shorts and T-shirts. The warmth is increasingly commonplace in March as temperatures rise with emissions.

video:video.video:title: Neighbor of man who rammed into Michigan synagogue reacts to attack
video:video.video:description: A neighbor of Ayman Mohamad Ghazali, who was killed by security after ramming a vehicle into Temple Israel in West Bloomfield Township near Detroit on Thursday, reacts to attack.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 13
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, March 13th: All six crew of US refueling plane are dead; Blast rocks Tehran after Israel threatened to target area of rally; Cuban President Díaz-Canel confirms talks with US; Markwayne Mullin faces tough DHS confirmation.

video:video.video:title: Democrats warn new Trump tariffs could cost families over $2,500 this year
video:video.video:description: President Trump is working to replace lost federal revenue after the Supreme Court struck down his tariffs.

video:video.video:title: How to read the AP election maps
video:video.video:description: It's the 2026 midterm season, with key state races drawing national attention as voters weigh the balance of power in Washington.

video:video.video:title: Iranian sailors’ bodies arrive at Mattala Airport in Sri Lanka ahead of repatriation
video:video.video:description: Sri Lanka's foreign ministry said Friday that the remains of 84 Iranian sailors killed in a U.S. submarine attack last week had been handed over to Iranian representatives to be repatriated.

video:video.video:title: Air raid sirens sound in Jerusalem as missile strikes Holon about 40 miles away
video:video.video:description: Air alert sirens sounded in Jerusalem again and a missile hit an open area on the Tel Aviv satellite town of Holon on Friday.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy offers countries assistance to combat Iranian drones he says Russia uses against Ukraine
video:video.video:description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Friday that Ukraine has received requests from six countries for drone combat assistance.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters and rescuers dispatched to site of Iranian missile hit in Central Israel
video:video.video:description: Firefighters and rescuers were dispatched to the location of an Iranian missile hit in an open area in Central Israel on Friday. (AP video by Sam Mednick)

video:video.video:title: Explosion rocks Tehran during government-organized rally, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: A large explosion rocked an area of Iran’s capital where thousands were gathered Friday for an annual state-organized rally to support the Palestinians and call for Israel’s demise.

video:video.video:title: Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of targeting civilians after fatal airstrikes
video:video.video:description: Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of targeting civilians after fatal airstrikes

video:video.video:title: Fire Engulfs Boat off Miami Beach
video:video.video:description: A 40 foot boat was on fire in Miami Beach, Florida on Thursday. There were no reported injuries, with Miami Beach Fire Department safely removing all occupants from the vessel.

video:video.video:title: 'Don't need to worry about it': Hegseth says Iran 'exercising sheer desperation' in Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says Iran is “exercising sheer desperation” in the Strait of Hormuz, which the war has effectively closed, blocking a significant portion of the world’s oil.

video:video.video:title: 5 indelible AP photos from Hollywood's awards season
video:video.video:description: As a staff photojournalist for The Associated Press, Chris Pizzello covers hundreds of entertainment events a year.

video:video.video:title: US Attorney Pirro to appeal judge's ruling against Fed subpoenas
video:video.video:description: Judge James Boasberg said that a “mountain of evidence suggests" that the purpose of the subpoenas was simply to pressure the Fed to cut its key interest rate, as President Donald Trump has repeatedly demanded.

video:video.video:title: At least 8 people killed in Israeli strike in Sidon, Lebanon
video:video.video:description: At least eight people have been killed in an Israeli strike in the southern coastal city of Sidon on Friday.

video:video.video:title: Family members of a man suspected of attacking a Michigan synagogue were killed in an Israeli airstrike last week, AP source says
video:video.video:description: Ayman Mohamad Ghazali lost two of his brothers, a niece and a nephew during an Israeli airstrike in Lebanon, an official said.

video:video.video:title: Workers in Tehran remove debris and rubble from buildings damaged by the Israeli-U.S. strikes
video:video.video:description: Workers in Tehran on Thursday removed debris and rubble from some of the buildings damaged by Israeli-U.S. strikes that hit the area of Resalat square earlier this week.

video:video.video:title: Oregon Army National Guard hoists injured logger to safety in Mount Hood National Forest
video:video.video:description: An Oregon Army National Guard helicopter safely hoisted an injured logger from a remote national forest on Thursday after steep, rugged terrain prevented other means of rescue, fire department officials said.

video:video.video:title: All but 2 of Austria's 96 glaciers have retreated over last 2 years, experts say
video:video.video:description: All but two of Austria’s 96 glaciers have retreated over the last two years, monitors in the Alpine country reported Friday, saying the “dramatic development” highlights the impact of climate change.

video:video.video:title: Brazil's telenovela industry is the secret agent behind powerful films at the Oscars
video:video.video:description: Hollywood actors might rule the silver screen — as Sunday’s Academy Awards is poised to prove — but Brazil’s path to stardom often starts under the bright lights of a TV studio rather than a sprawling movie set.

video:video.video:title: Injured logger rescued from Mount Hood National Forest
video:video.video:description: An Oregon Army National Guard helicopter safely hoisted an injured logger from Mount Hood National Forest, after steep, rugged terrain prevented other means of rescue.

video:video.video:title: Indigenous women tour Ecuador oil field as warning against Amazon drilling expansion
video:video.video:description: About 30 Indigenous women from seven Amazon nationalities traveled to Ecuador’s northern oil region to witness the environmental impacts of decades of oil and gas extraction, part of a workshop aimed at preparing communities for possible expansion of drilling.

video:video.video:title: FBI says the man who attacked a Michigan synagogue died from a self-inflicted gunshot wound
video:video.video:description: Authorities say 41-year-old Ayman Mohamad Ghazali waited in his car outside a Detroit-area synagogue for two hours before ramming into the building.

video:video.video:title: Germany's Merz, Canada's Carney disagree with US' 30-day waiver on Russian oil sanctions
video:video.video:description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said Friday that all other G7 countries disagreed with a U.S. move to ease sanctions on Russian oil.

video:video.video:title: Scream clubs: A vocal release for stress
video:video.video:description: Can you scream your way to happiness? Maybe not, but the scream clubs popping up around the U.S. promise some relief. (AP Video: Emilie Megnien, Laura Bargfeld)

video:video.video:title: US eases some Russian oil sanctions but crude prices stay high
video:video.video:description: The U.S. is temporarily easing some sanctions on Russian oil shipments, reflecting global concerns over sharply higher crude prices due to supply shortages stemming from the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Iranian missile hits Holon in central Israel with no reported casualties, AP explains
video:video.video:description: An Iranian missile struck a site in central Israel Friday evening. There were no casualties recorded as a result of the attacks throughout the day, according to authorities.

video:video.video:title: AP Reporter at site of Iranian strike in Central Israel
video:video.video:description: AP Reporter Sam Mednick was in central Israel at a site where an Iranian missile struck Friday. This came as Israel and the US continued their attacks on Iran.

video:video.video:title: Rescue workers remove rubble after a building in Isfahan is hit by US-Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: Iranian state television broadcast footage Friday on its website showing damage to buildings and cars following a U.S.

video:video.video:title: ‘The Secret Agent’ director on Oscars in wartime
video:video.video:description: "The Secret Agent" writer-director Kleber Mendonça Filho says it's a balancing act for filmmakers to celebrate at the Oscars during a war in the Middle East. (March 13)

video:video.video:title: Indigenous women tour Ecuador oil field as warning against Amazon drilling expansion
video:video.video:description: About 30 Indigenous women from seven Amazon nationalities traveled to Ecuador’s northern oil region to witness the environmental impacts of decades of oil and gas extraction, part of a workshop aimed at preparing communities for possible expansion of drilling.

video:video.video:title: Lebanon's largest sports stadium turned into shelter for people displaced by conflict - AP Explains
video:video.video:description: In just 10 days, more than 800,000 people in Lebanon have been displaced by war, just over a year since the last conflict uprooted over a million from their homes.

video:video.video:title: Smoke, fire seen coming from US embassy compound in Baghdad after reported missile strike
video:video.video:description: A missile struck a helipad inside the U.S. Embassy compound in Baghdad, two Iraqi security officials said.
